text.title: go-error-handling Core Rules
text.summary: Nine testable rules for typed Go errors, wrapping, boundary mapping, and middleware order.

rule.statement: Define AppError with Code, Message, HTTPStatus, and Err fields. Implement Error() and Unwrap() so errors.Is/As work across wrapping chains.
rule.rationale: Without Unwrap(), errors.As fails to traverse the chain; type assertions break on the first wrap.

rule.statement: Always wrap with fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err) — never %v. Use errors.As at HTTP boundaries, never type assertions.
rule.rationale: %v stringifies and drops the chain; errors.As is the chain-aware comparator.

rule.statement: Map driver errors (pgx.ErrNoRows, sql.ErrNoRows, redis.Nil) to AppError at the repository layer. Never let driver errors reach HTTP handlers.
rule.rationale: Driver-specific types create implicit coupling at the wrong layer; handlers cannot know which driver is below them.

rule.statement: Use a Handler type (func returning error) + Wrap adapter so handlers can return errors directly instead of writing responses inline. One middleware logs and translates.
rule.rationale: Inline response writes duplicate the translation logic and inevitably drift.

rule.statement: Register panic-recovery middleware BEFORE the error-mapping middleware. Panics that reach the error mapper produce blank 500s with no body.
rule.rationale: Recovery must convert panic to error first; if mapping runs first, the panic bypasses it.

rule.statement: Always call w.WriteHeader BEFORE json.NewEncoder(w).Encode. Writing the body first flushes an implicit 200.
rule.rationale: http.ResponseWriter flushes the status on first body byte; the status set later is ignored.

rule.statement: Run golangci-lint with errorlint, wrapcheck, errcheck, and nilnil enabled. Default config misses most error-handling bugs.
rule.rationale: Defaults don't catch %v wrapping or unwrapped returns; explicit linter set encodes the rules.

rule.statement: Never return package-level mutable *AppError vars. Use constructor functions (NewNotFound, NewBadRequest) that return new instances.
rule.rationale: `ErrNotFound.Message = "X"` mutates the global; one request corrupts state for every subsequent request.

rule.statement: When wrapping at a higher layer, preserve the inner AppError's HTTPStatus and Code when present. A 404 must not become 500 on rewrap.
rule.rationale: Status demotion on rewrap hides client errors as server errors and breaks SLO accounting.

rule.statement: AppError shape, %w wrapping, repo-boundary mapping, one middleware, panic-then-map order, lint config — all green.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the decision tree's positive branch.

rule.statement: No HTTP layer, or Go < 1.13 — methodology does not apply.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the decision tree's negative branch.
